steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "a cultural studies analysis ______ on the intersection of literature and popular culture might yield a radically different interpretation." The main verb is "might yield" — the blank must modify "analysis" without adding a second competing verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "would focus": Creates a second finite verb ("would focus") with no conjunction joining it to "might yield," making the sentence ungrammatical. ✗
  • "would be focusing": Same problem — another finite verb phrase competing with "might yield." ✗
  • "focusing": A participial phrase that modifies "analysis" without adding a second main verb. The sentence reads smoothly: "a cultural studies analysis focusing on the intersection... might yield..." ✓
  • "focuses": A third-person singular verb that again competes with "might yield" as a main verb. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
